---
title: "Curriculum Vitae"
---

[Download PDF](assets/delouis-cv.pdf){.btn .btn-primary role="button"}

## Education

**PhD in Social Science** (Department of Sociology), University of Zürich — *05/2025 – present*<br> 

**Master in Politics and Policy Analysis**, Bocconi University, Milan — *09/2022 – 10/2024*<br> Double degree, in parallel with AgroParisTech. Thesis: *Marching Together? Military Conscription and Immigration Attitudes in Europe.*<br> Coursework: Methods for Policy Analysis, Welfare and Public Economics, Politics of Conflict, Citizenship and Migration.

**Master in Environmental Management and Engineering**, AgroParisTech, Paris-Saclay — *09/2020 – 11/2024*

**Erasmus+ in Sustainable Development Law**, Università degli Studi di Milano — *02/2021 – 07/2021*

**Bachelor of Engineering**, AgroParisTech, Paris-Saclay — *09/2019 – 08/2020*

**Classes préparatoires BCPST** (Biology, Chemistry, Physics, Earth Sciences), Lycée Georges Clemenceau, Nantes — *09/2017 – 07/2019*

## Research visits

**Visiting PhD Researcher**, Stockholm University — *02/2026 – 03/2026*<br> Six weeks. Data access and collaboration on a comparative naturalisation project (Sweden and the Netherlands).

## Research experience

**Pre-Doctoral Fellow**, Bocconi University, Milan — *10/2024 – 04/2025*

-   Research assistance on projects in political science using advanced statistical and computational methods.
-   Developed data collection strategies leveraging large language models and the Facebook Marketing API for demographic and migration data.
-   Built computational pipelines for integrating online and offline data sources.
-   Assisted in designing experimental and observational studies.

**Research Assistant** (with Ala Alrababah), Bocconi University, Milan — *04/2024 – 09/2024*

-   Data management and analysis for research on comparative politics and international relations, focusing on immigration, refugees, and political violence.
-   Contributed to developing research projects employing observational, experimental, and computational methods.

**Research Trainee**, OECD Development Centre, Centre on Philanthropy, Paris — *02/2024 – 08/2024*

-   Co-authored thematic research on philanthropy and gender equality.
-   Designed and piloted the next edition of the OECD Private Philanthropy for Development global survey.
-   Developed a machine learning model to classify philanthropic financing for climate adaptation.
-   Drafted briefing notes, concept papers, and funding proposals.
-   Streamlined the open data workflow of the Centre on Philanthropy.

## Other professional experience

**Engineer Intern**, Glimpact, Brussels — *11/2021 – 05/2022*

-   Applied the Product Environmental Footprint (PEF) methodology; conducted Life-Cycle Assessment studies.

**Intern, PNSE 4 Implementation and Air Quality**, Ministry of Ecological Transition (DGPR), Paris-La Défense — *06/2021 – 11/2021*

-   Conducted research on environmental health policy and air quality management strategies.
-   Assisted in regulatory review and coordination with municipal representatives.

## Publications

See [Research](research.qmd) for working papers and work in progress.

**Policy publications**

-   OECD (2024). *How Philanthropic Foundations Support Gender Equality and Women's Empowerment.* OECD Development Policy Papers, No. 55. [DOI](https://doi.org/10.1787/3fbec947-en)

## Conference presentations

-   **2026**  International Migration, Integration and Social Cohesion (IMISCOE) Annual Conference
-   **2026**  European Political Science Society (EPSS) Annual Conference
-   **2026**  Visions in Methodology (VIM) — *Poster Award* for *The Army of Unity? Conscription and the Making of Belonging in Reunified Germany*
-   **2025**  European Political Science Association (EPSA) Annual Conference

## Service and volunteering

-   **Student Representative**, Junior Researchers — *08/2026 – present*
-   **Fundraising Manager**, later **Advisory Board Member**, Northern Lights Aid (remote) — *01/2024 – present*
-   **Volunteer**, Refugee Welcome International and Italy / Opera Cardinal Ferrari, Milan — *09/2023 – present*
-   **Field Volunteer and Sensibility Trainer**, Northern Lights Aid / Mothers Without Borders, Kavala, Greece — *06/2023 – 07/2023*
-   **Field Volunteer**, Women For The Earth, Abidjan, Côte d'Ivoire — *06/2022 – 08/2022*

## Skills

**Languages**: French (native), English (C1), Italian (B2), German (B2), Spanish (B2)

**Programming**: R (Quarto, Shiny), Python, Stata, SQL, C, MATLAB

**Methods**: Machine learning, text analysis, web scraping, data visualisation, causal inference
